If your organization has more than one target, list the additional ones here. Include interim targets, targets that have differing dates or reduction percentages than those listed in the previous questions.

Interim targets refer to those that should be completed before the timeframe of your major target and serve as interim milestones to achieving the major target(s).

There are 15 categories of Scope 3 emissions according to the GHG Protocol. See link for more details: \n\nMany organizations only are reporting Scope 3 data for a few of these categories. It is common to only report on Upstream emissions (i.e. Categories 1-8). Please select all of the categories that encompass your Scope 3 reported emissions in the previous question.
In your reported Scope 3 emissions above, please indicate which of the standard categories (as according to the GHG Protocol) are included.
Category 1: Purchased goods and services
Category 10: Processing of sold products
Category 11: Use of sold products
Category 12: End-of-life treatment of sold products
Category 13: Downstream leased assets
Category 14: Franchises
Category 15: Investments
Category 2: Capital goods
Category 3: Fuel- and energy-related activities (not included in Scope 1 or 2)
Category 4: Upstream transportation and distribution
Category 5: Waste generated in operations
Category 6: Business travel
Category 7: Employee commuting
Category 8: Upstream leased assets
Category 9: Downstream transportation and distribution

This question refers to your organization’s use of recycled materials in any part of your production process, whether in raw materials, packaging, components, or finished goods.\n\nExamples include recycled plastics, metals, paper, textiles, or other recovered inputs. Please answer based on current practices, not future goals.
Does your organization use recycled materials in your production process?
Yes, used significantly in core materials or components across many products (e.g. estimated recycled content in >50% of SKUs or across >50% of production volume)
Not applicable - we do not produce physical goods or materials
Other
Yes, used significantly in core materials or components but in only some products (e.g. >50% recycled materials in select product lines)
Yes, used moderately in core materials or components across product categories/types (e.g. approximately 10-50% recycled content)
Yes, used moderately in core materials or components but only in some products (e.g. 10-50% recycled materials in select product lines)
Yes, recycled content used, but total volume is limited (estimated <10% of material inputs)
Yes, used in packaging across most or all products
Yes, used in packaging for select product lines
Not currently using recycled materials, but actively exploring
Not currently using recycled materials and no plans at this stage

A transition plan is a strategic roadmap to outline how your organization will shift its operations, assets, and business model to align with a low-carbon or net-zero future - typically consistent with global climate goals like limiting warming to 1.5°C.\n\nAdditional guidance on transition planning is available here.
Does your organization have a transition plan in place to outline how your organization will shift to align with a low-carbon or net-zero future?
Yes, a formally documented and active transition plan with defined targets and timelines for Scope 1 and 2 emissions
Yes, a formally documented and active transition plan with defined targets and timelines for Scope 1, 2 and 3 emissions
We have a draft or informal plan that is being developed or refined
We do not currently have a plan but intend to develop one within the next 12 months
No current plan or timeline to create one
